data.body:
PASS 1 plan-review (T5c — Security overlay). The overlay APPLIES: this plan stands up real security surface — a public HTTPS review-bot endpoint (`/review/` on 443, nginx→127.0.0.1:${REVIEW_BOT_PORT}) plus Gerrit HTTP 8080 / SSH 29418, an authn boundary on inbound webhooks, and multiple credentials/grants (a GitHub replication deploy key, a Gerrit bot HTTP token, an EC2 instance role reading SSM SecureStrings, KMS decrypt). Verdict: sound on 3 of the 4 sub-checks; 2 grounded coaching findings, both on data-at-rest / secret-in-logs. (These independently corroborate the two prior T5c reviews on this ticket — I re-verified both against the live infra/ code and they still hold.)

AFFIRMATIONS (T5c criteria this plan satisfies):
- (a) Endpoint access levels declared + sensitive paths gated: the access model is explicit and sound. `/review/` on 443 is the only public surface; the stdio MCP is deliberately NOT exposed over HTTP. The webhook path is a two-layer control — PRIMARY is internal-only container-to-container delivery over the private compose network (webhooks.config:14-19: receiver host-port loopback-bound `127.0.0.1:8000`, port 8000 NOT in the security group, so unreachable except via nginx `/review/`); SECONDARY is the URL-embedded token with receiver-side validation. The "webhooks plugin has NO HMAC" limitation is named honestly (webhooks.config:12; Round-11 note) rather than pretending a signature exists. Per-`(change_id, revision)` single-flight + idempotency guard the review/vote critical section against retries/backfill. IMDSv2 is required (main.tf:50-54) — defends the instance-role creds against SSRF theft.
- (c) LEAST-PRIVILEGE on new grants: strong. The replication deploy key is single-repo WRITE-only to GitHub and is the sole ruleset bypass actor; the bot HTTP token's dual scope (REST `/a/` + git-over-HTTPS read) is documented + single-sourced; the SSM read grant is scoped to the `/rebar/prod/*` PREFIX, explicitly "NOT Resource:\"*\"" (iam_s4a.tf:12-18), with kms:Decrypt for the SecureString; the IAM-role ownership contract (S1 CREATES the role; S2/S4a only ADD separately-named scoped inline policies, "never a second role") prevents over-grant/clobber. No wildcard `*:*` / admin-for-convenience.
- (d) Secret lifecycle (largely): AWS SSM Parameter Store SecureString pulled to a `chmod 600` `.env` at boot; the enumerated SSM param set is completeness-checked (`/rebar/prod/alert-endpoint` + `/rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token`). No plaintext secrets committed to IaC (verified: `infra/gerrit/webhooks.config:23` uses a `__BOT_TOKEN__` placeholder substituted at boot, not a literal). See findings below for the two secret-lifecycle gaps that keep this from a clean (d).

FINDINGS (2):

- [ ] Specify encryption-at-rest (`encrypted = true` + KMS) for the dedicated `/var/gerrit` NoteDb EBS volume AND its DLM snapshots. The "NoteDb persistence + backup" bullet / AC1 names only "a dedicated gp3 EBS volume at `/var/gerrit` with `prevent_destroy` + DLM daily snapshots", with NO at-rest encryption statement — and the code confirms the gap: `aws_ebs_volume "data"` (infra/terraform/main.tf:27-40) sets `type = "gp3"` + `prevent_destroy` but declares NO `encrypted` argument, and a repo-wide `encrypted` search under infra/ returns ZERO matches (so even the root device is unencrypted, contra derisk note E1's "gp3-encrypted" claim). This volume holds `refs/meta/config`, where the live webhook bot token is stored (ADR-0014), and DLM snapshots INHERIT the source volume's (currently absent) encryption.
  - criteria: [T5c]
  - location: Decomposition & resolved design questions → "NoteDb persistence + backup" bullet; AC1; infra/terraform/main.tf:27-40
  - evidence: plan phrase "NoteDb on a dedicated gp3 EBS volume at `/var/gerrit` with `prevent_destroy` + DLM daily snapshots (S1)"; code fact — `aws_ebs_volume.data` (main.tf:27-40) declares no `encrypted`, and `search infra/ 'encrypted'` → 0 hits; the token-bearing `refs/meta/config` lives on this volume; T5c(b) at-rest encryption where sensitive data is stored.
  - suggested_fix: Add `encrypted = true` (AWS-managed key or a project CMK) to `aws_ebs_volume.data` (and the `root_block_device`); state in S1's AC that encryption propagates to the DLM snapshots so the token-bearing `refs/meta/config` is encrypted at rest.

- [ ] Address the token-in-query-string log-leak vector. The bot token travels as a `?token=<token>` query parameter on every webhook POST (`infra/gerrit/webhooks.config:23`: `url = http://review-bot:8000/webhook?token=__BOT_TOKEN__`) AND on the PUBLIC operator `/review/rerun?token=$TOKEN` path (`infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md:34`, over `https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/...`). Query strings are a classic secret-in-logs sink (nginx access log, receiver request log, Gerrit webhooks-plugin delivery log). The plan/ADR-0014 bound the token's exposure in `refs/meta/config` but do NOT state that `?token=` is redacted from access/request logs — and the nginx `/review/` location (infra/nginx/rebar.conf.template:54-60) sets no `access_log off`/redaction, while a repo-wide `access_log` search under infra/ returns ZERO matches (no log config pinned), so the default combined log records the full URI including the token.
  - criteria: [T5c]
  - location: Round-11 remediation → inbound-auth note ("inbound auth is a secret-token URL"); infra/gerrit/webhooks.config:23; infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md:34; infra/nginx/rebar.conf.template:54-60
  - evidence: code fact — the token is in the URL query on both the webhook and the public `/rerun` path; T5c(d) "no secret-logging"; ABSENCE of any log-redaction statement for `?token=` across the nginx/receiver/webhooks-plugin log path (`search infra/ 'access_log'` → 0 hits).
  - suggested_fix: State the redaction posture in S4a — e.g. nginx `/review/` logs a redacted URI (map the request URI to strip `token=…`, or set `access_log off` on that location) and the receiver masks the token in its request log; OR move the token to an HTTP header if the plugin supports one, keeping only the network-ACL primary gate in the URL.
